name: Maya Angelou
description: American poet, author, and civil rights activist (1928–2014)
abstract: Maya Angelou (  AN-jə-loh; born Marguerite Annie Johnson; April 4, 1928 – May 28, 2014) was an American memoirist, essayist, poet, and civil rights activist. She published seven autobiographies, three books of essays, several books of poetry, and is credited with a list of plays, movies, and television shows spanning over 50 years.

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: We were able to convince Maya Angelou to – it didn’t take much. She would allow us to put music to one of her poems.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ruthie Foster
about_quotes.evidence: Some of my favorite poets were Dr. Maya Angelou... I actually got a chance to talk to and see [Nikki Giovanni]... Maya Angelou was huge in my life during that period.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruthie Foster interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 28, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Maya and I were talking about that. ... We had so much fun. ... Maya grabbed a 80-year-old Afrikaner afterwards. ... Maya held him
